If Patriot betrays Talulah, what happens then?
...That won't happen. Patriot trusts Talulah deeply.
Now what about the other way around?
...Dr. Kal'tsit?!
What if Talulah betrays Patriot?
Or, what if I told you we encountered the Reunion commander, FrostNova, and by any objective measure, are responsible for her death?
Dr. Kal'tsit?! What are you saying?!
That... th-that's Patriot's daughter...!
If we fight Patriot...
That would be the worst case scenario. And it's what the puppetmasters would want.
If Talulah really is turning a blind eye to, or even allowing the Infected to harm innocent civilians in spite of Patriot's stance... I believe that's no longer just turning a blind eye.
A lot of Infected will make Patriot into a target. Those who don't know the truth, and those who let their hatred get the better of them.
And those who want to.
I... I have to go.
I have to tell Patriot!
But... Rhodes Island...
Will he be... your enemy?

opts: I didn't think things would turn out like this.

lines.x: It's not the obvious step for Reunion to take.
Whether Misha or FrostNova...
Us, Reunion, and Lungmen too... We can't let anyone else slip through the cracks.
Doctor, we still have a lot to do... just thinking of what comes next is making me tired.
But before that... we have to find Madame Ch'en.
And, most importantly...
The leader of Reunion. She's the cause of all of this.
Right. Team Mantra-2, come in.
The captain has determined Reunion's communication method. It's primitive, even artificial-looking. As for personnel, she's just about done clearing the area.
Not the guerrillas though. They're tough.
Should we eliminate them?
No need for that. We're not here to eliminate them, or have them butcher each other.
We're not here to stir up internal conflict. They should oppose those who misled them.
Now?
That's her question. I'm good.
Naturally... not now.
Their spirit is rotten, but their fabrication of a backbone still remains.
Take it away or break it, and watch them crumble. That's what we might have done at first.
Talulah was a step ahead of us. She stirred the conflict and fanned its flames.
I originally thought that she had gone mad. But this is poison, not madness.
The Infected and the people of the city will surely rise up.
Talulah gave them the chance to intermingle and build bridges.
She must still have a queen on the board, or she wouldn't be so bold.
I firmly believe that she wants to destroy Reunion, in the most dishonorable possible way.
...And this is a poison that we must neutrallize.
But all this hinges on what Buldrokkas'tee decides to do...

opts: It looks like we've successfully infiltrated the core city.

lines.x: Because what we do next can hardly be called an infiltration, Dr. Doctor.
As I said when we started, infiltration is only about twenty percent of our plan here.
To get to the core command tower and the generator area to halt Chernobog's movement, we will have to go through the central district.
Now, the enemies in our path are not your run-of-the-mill Reunion, nor are they brutish Sarkaz sellswords.
Let's put W aside for now.
As a tactician, you should be more concerned about this than about Talulah, Reunion's tyrant.
The Infected Guerrillas of the Northern Plains, a thorn in the hinterland of Ursus's Fourth Army to the northwest... Since its formation, it has only ever had one leader.
Ordinary resistance fighters get slaughtered, but these guerrillas were able to turn the tables on the butchers.
Local stakeholders could request the mobilization of a division or two to knock them out, but it would be taken as an admission of incompetence to the generals and the State Council.
The war raged for so long that the Infected hiding out in the snow came to call the guerrillas their 'Aegis.'
The Aegis of the Infected.
The Yeti Squad was one of its subsets, and its members did not solely consist of Infected.
The exiles, the fanatics, the maimed, Elves, Durin, and Sarkaz. The guerrillas did not fight for the Infected alone.
Oh?

opts: I just said whatever came to mind.

lines.x: Just like Rhodes Island, yes?
Just as before, Rhodes Island will benefit from your intuition, Dr. Doctor.

opts: I thought you wouldn't appreciate that.

lines.x: Why do you look into my eyes when you say that?
There's nothing to see in these eyes.
There's no point in staying silent at a time like this.
It's your choice whether or not you hold an opinion.
Would you rather I didn't?
You want me to reprimand you, Dr. Doctor?
Not now. I don't think you do, and I don't think there's anything wrong with what you said.
In an unexpectedly apt analogy, the guerrilla fighters also trust only one commander, and embrace only that one commander.
Yes, Doctor, this guerrilla force is another sort of 'Rhodes Island.'
But their leader? No.
Dr. Doctor, prepare yourself well. Or you'll regret it.
Even though one of our objectives is to clear a path through the central district to the bridge and the generator area...
For your own safety, I still hope you and Amiya will not face him.
But if this is all inevitable, then I can only repeat what I just said.
...Prepare yourself well, Dr. Doctor. And before you face him, you must prepare to face his guerrillas.
I rarely offer my advice to others, Doctor.
But that is 'Patriot,' Buldrokkas'tee.
*Pant*... *pant*...
Still... another... block?
No... No! I have to... I have to tell Patriot...
If Talulah... if Talulah lets these people... strike at the guerrillas...
Then... then... the guerrillas will... they will...
Be killed by their own...!
Patriot... Patriot is the only level-headed one left in Reunion...!
Where do you think you're going?
You...
Sarkaz.
What brigade are you with? And what business do you have in the central district?
Explain it to me.
Oh, or maybe you're with the old Wendigo.
You—
The prey ran right up to me on his own.
What did you learn, weak little Infected outlander?
Chernobog Central District 1:20 P.M.
Raidian, this is Kal'tsit. We're on the edge of the central district, four hundred and ten meters out from the warehouse, seventeen, sixty-seven, southwest.
Are you in position? Is the operation going according to plan?
'Unimpeded?'
The situation on the ground seems to be in line with our expectations.
Reunion has deployed the guerrilla fighters at hubs throughout the core city, and stationed Reunion regulars or Sarkaz mercenaries elsewhere.
It would be unwise to spread their defenses thinly across the entire map. They will gather their elites in a few key locations. That's what I would do if I were leading Reunion.
Dr. Kal'tsit!
I know you want to speak to Raidian, Amiya. Patience.
I... I do too...
...We're on a mission. Next time, okay?
Oh, um... okay.
Raidian, I have Amiya here for you.
Right!
Raidian, this is Amiya.
(Well... You saw how Amiya and Rosmontis reacted.)
(Our younger personnel love Raidian. But she may be a little too warm and friendly for adults.)
(The adolescents have the most trouble with her.)
(Their need to self-actualize tends to conflict with Raidian's persistent motherliness.)
(I generally avoid putting them in the same team. You should check the manual for such details when planning.)
Right... I saw, Raidian, yes.
That's correct. No, that's not a standard tactic for guerrilla fighters. It's more like... hm...
Yes! Like the Doctor said, Raidian, Reunion has mixed things up a bit, but you can still see the shadow of the Ursus military here.
They've undergone strict military training... Yes, that's likely.
The core network of Reunion personnel could be Infected who have received Ursus military training.
They're the only ones who could gather up such a large group of Infected.
It's clear that this battle won't be an easy win for us.
Hm? No, I'm fine. Rosmontis and I, all the Rhodes Island operators... we all wanted to be here.
Uh huh, it's okay, Raidian, I have Dr. Doctor and Dr. Kal'tsit with me.
All right, Amiya, it's time to begin.
Right. Okay, Raidian, it's up to you and Dr. Kal'tsit.
First thing's first, we have to make good use of our comms.
Team R-4, activate your equipment.
Raidian, it will take Rosmontis ten minutes to cast her Arts. During that time, we cannot lose comms for even a split second, unless we determine that we have to disconnect.
Continued use of Originium Arts will cause you discomfort, and if the nausea becomes unbearable, you are to immediately take the supplement I formulated for you.
Moderate dizziness is a side effect. It won't harm your nervous system, but it will massively drain your strength.
Be careful not to overdose. After the first ten minutes, you can take intermittent breaks. Do not overexert yourself.
We're asking a lot of you, Raidian.
Rosmontis, all of the enemy's messages will be sent to your portable terminal via Raidian. Feel them, and log them.
Should their location change, you cannot lose them. Seize their feelings and log them again.
Reach out with your body, Rosmontis, and picture yourself roaming the city.
I understand, Dr. Kal'tsit.
Amiya...
I know.
You have your work cut out for you. If anything anomalous happens, I will stop your casting. The instant you feel any pain, let me know.
Rosmontis... I'm going closer to you.
Yes, I'm ready too. I'm not scared. Amiya, don't be scared of me.
I won't. I'm not scared of Rosmontis. We won't be scared.
Amiya is stabilizing Rosmontis's emotions.
You may not want to look directly at them, Dr. Doctor. It is unwise for any but the most tragically afflicted warriors to look directly at the expansion of the essence of Rosmontis's Arts.

opts: You can't mean our little bunny...?

lines.x: I figured you'd realize it sooner.
Or that you already knew but hadn't indicated as much to me.
But yes, Dr. Doctor.
...Amiya's battles, both physical and mental, have been far beyond what any human being should have to endure.
Two, or maybe three would be okay. The more people to share the burden, the better.
Dr. Doctor, I'm not talking specifically about a memory or a battlefield experience. This refers to demands on both responsibility and physical ability.
If you'd like, you could hold Amiya's hand to share that burden.
No, not today, anyway. You and I have another urgent task.
We need to buy them precious time.
A pharmaceutical company investigates the root cause of a problem, adapts to the environment, allocates functions appropriately, and looks for a breakthrough.
Our target is smaller and more precise than in a conventional battle. A small tactical assault team's work is closer to a surgery than it is to a firefight.
What the warmongers can't do falls within our area of expertise.
Rhodes Island is going to take out the enemy command in the core city. All our objectives center on rooting out the cause of this disease.
But even if we excise the cancer, if the cause of the problem remains unknown to us, it will not be fundamentally solved.
Dr. Doctor, I do hope you will give this due consideration.
—Who are our patients?
The normal Infected? The Ursus who were Infected? Reunion? Or this city?
And where is the cause of this disease?
We've discussed how Reunion is not maintaining the city's communication networks.
It has become clear that Talulah deliberately kept Originium clusters from the Catastrophe in the city to maintain a state of chaos.
That will massively disrupt our communications.
Even our most advanced equipment will not function under these conditions.
But now, thanks to elite operator Raidian, we can make use of the otherwise chaotic airwaves.
That leaves us with a direct, reliable method of communication, and a tactical advantage.
True.
You saw the runners carrying megaphones. The guerrillas use these antiquated methods to carry intelligence and orders in encrypted messages.
We have yet to properly map the movements of these runners, but Rosmontis is working on it.
Their transmissions may not be instantaneous, and are limited in range, but it is the most stable and disciplined method in a hostile environment.
Can you guess what's wrong with this method?

opts: It depends on the person first giving the order?
The squad can't survive without its head.

lines.x: Correct.
The real commander's location is hidden.
What would you do in that position, Doctor?

opts: I'd have to cause some chaos first.

lines.x: Correct.
Now we've focused our thinking. This is another tactical advantage.
Doctor, the Arts Rosmontis is releasing will show us the location of every runner and their patrol routes.
She will expand her consciousness and capture every physical structure within range. At the same time, her mental state will grow increasingly chaotic.
Amiya is working to flatten those emotions.
Raidian is the core of our communications. With her Arts, we can communicate unimpeded for a short period of time.
We just need to wait for Rosmontis to finish her part, then we go.
For now, the guerrillas know nothing about us.
They are completely unaware that we are targeting their communications, or that we are able to work against the ancient Sarkaz witchcraft..
We do have our methods, limited as they may be.
After striking the runners, the guerrillas will respond. Within a half hour, they will have new runners working, and they will be hardened against further attacks.
They will be on high alert, and may not have weaknesses to exploit.
What's more, we will have exposed our positioning and tactics, putting us in a difficult situation.
However, we only need to execute a single surgical strike. With that one blow, we will force out their command unit.
The guerrillas won't be expecting another Infected spec ops team.
This is a tactical advantage.

opts: I'm more concerned with implementation. Will it work?

lines.x: —I'm not going to exaggerate.
Dr. Doctor, even with our three tactical advantages, we are more likely to fail than succeed.

opts: Then why do it like this?

lines.x: Because anything else would be even less likely to succeed.
...Rosmontis should have started her search. Hold your breath, and don't look.
—First—
I... found three.
Rosmontis... you're okay. Come here, a little closer.
...Four.
Your ear. I'm running my finger over your ear like this... you can hear it. These are just fragments of your imagination, and you've seen it all before.
Mm...
Five.
Doctor, are you afraid of getting hurt?
These three teams will split up for the fight. Each will be responsible for enemy teams and crippling the runners in a different sector. We cannot give them a chance to get the word out.
Each of our squads will also redistribute their power projection, which I have already planned for. Don't worry.
As for you, you're with me.
Bring your security specialist. You can't refuse. I think.
The guerrillas' equipment is of excellent quality, similar to that of the Ursus military, and they keep to discipline and tactics even more harshly and effectively.
Sarkaz, Ursus, Liberi and other races all united under a single banner.
You are safe only under my protection. I don't want Rosmontis to kill you by accident, and I don't want Amiya to distract herself trying to protect you. And Raidian, you haven't even met.
You can't cause trouble with me.
...Wait.
—Rosmontis?
Ah... Nng...
Raidian, sever Rosmontis's connection. Now!
Amiya, her mental state?
But... Rosmontis... The scene I reached out to was stable, nothing strange was happening... It shouldn't have...
It's... falling? She's... looking at the sky?
...Lost. She's lost. She's falling.
Body temperature dropping, shortness of breath... Sharp decrease in endorphin levels.
(Shakes her head)
This isn't like Raidian using supplements to replenish her energy. Rosmontis's Arts throw off a balance inside her body, but not an entirely biological one.
Injecting steroids to increase hormone production can seriously disrupt her endocrine system that could result in a failure of her inhibitor functions.
You left me? Why... I have to...
—Why did you leave me...?
Bad is not an excuse to indulge my incompetence.
Dr. Doctor, I will not resort to emergency measures that cause permanent bodily harm... no matter how effective they may be.
Rosmontis's problem is not biological. She is the only one who can solve it.
Rosmontis.
Rosmontis, look at my hand.
Doc... tor... Kal... tsit..
Look at Dr. Kal'tsit's hand.
My hand is stroking your face now. Look, Dr. Kal'tsit is here. Look at me.
...Hnng.
Rosmontis, Kal'tsit doesn't have much time to stay with you. Neither does Amiya...
Outcast, Misery, Blaze, or anyone else.
But for now, Amiya and I are here.
Uh... huh.
Keep pushing, Rosmontis. Walk forward.
Yes, Rosmontis... over here. Walk this way. On the deck.
It's very hard, I know... But, Rosmontis, this is the deck, you can step on it. You won't fall through.
This is Rhodes Island.
We're here with you.
I'm here with you.
—I know.
...Doctor... Kal'tsit... give me...
The... data.
—Okay.
Raidian, connect Rosmontis's terminal. She's okay.
I'm sure. Do it.
—Second.
Seven.
I see..
Her... Rosmontis is standing now. Her consciousness is peaceful and harmonious. Aw... it's like a fluffy basket holding her dreams.
We're fine, Dr. Kal'tsit.
Pfft.
Amiya's turning red, Dr. Doctor.
...I didn't want you to see that!
It's okay. Mmhm. Okay.
Again... again. Amiya, let's go forward a bit. I can feel it.
Third.
...Dr. Kal'tsit, I found them. All of them.
We're ready.
Yes, thank you. And thank you, Amiya.
Don't thank me for doing my job, Rosmontis... no need. You know I love saying that, don't you?
Uh huh. But you took me to the bridge, and to the mountain... and the clouds were so pretty.
It's a long road.
Let's go, Amiya. We can go down.
Raidian, split the connections. You can take five.
Amiya, Rosmontis, keep the channel clear.
Okay, now, Raidian, Dr. Kal'tsit, Rosmontis...
Time to fight.
It looks like you have something on your mind.

opts: Your tone is much softer than it used to be.

lines.x: That's not what you're thinking. But if you'd like, I can be harsher with you.

opts: I want to talk about the silver-haired girl.

lines.x: You were very lucky back there.
Taking a step back, it may be that Rosmontis likes you. She controlled her outburst.
Otherwise you would have bitten off your own tongue watching Amiya and I guide her, and seeing the manifestation of her Arts.
...Rosmontis was born in a lab.
She did not suffer the same tragedy as most experimental Infected. She did not die from the increasingly cruel experiments.
As the project moved forward, she gradually crushed the entire facility.
The lab did not destroy her, she destroyed it. Her Arts are tied to her mental state.
The group that found her and smuggled her to Rhodes Island has made it clear that 'Rosmontis does not exist.'
I know what you're thinking. And no, through force or coercion, I would never allow an individual who has been through that to become an operator.
But Rosmontis herself chose responsibility, training, and companionship.
With her subconscious Arts usage causing constant damage, the caster operators decided to train her. She had to mature faster than the others, which she fortunately wanted to do.
We didn't want to abandon her to the wilderness. We did not want to inject her with suppressants. And we certainly didn't want Rhodes Island to be reduced to a heap of pulverized shrapnel.
This solution was our last resort. We have to train her to use her Arts, rather than indulge or imprison her.
She's like Amiya in some ways, and unlike her in others.
Then let me finish, please.
They are alike in that they will both fight to the end for other people.
As for how they're different—
You know Amiya has never wanted to make enemies. Even when forced to do so, she fights back her tears, reluctantly destroying her enemies.
But Rosmontis, I could say, Doctor, that Rosmontis...
She's never given up on a mission. Faced with any situation, she handles it appropriate, and wins.
That is our simultaneously reasonable and incomprehensible Annihilation Specialist, Rosmontis.
She is an elite operator for no other reason than she met the standards across the entire battery of tests.
The operators who feared her came to know what she was really like and ultimately put their lives in her hands...
Because she protects them, with all her power.
If Blaze won the respect of our people through leadership of her team, Rosmontis fought for their respect.
She heard their expectations, and exceeded them.

opts: But there are practical considerations to her participation here.

lines.x: There are other operators with detection Arts similar to Rosmontis's, with different casting conditions.
Rosmontis can do more than just locate, you've seen the way she fights.
Dr. Doctor, we allowed Rosmontis to join this op because she is the best operator for the job, and because she believes as much herself.
Her Arts are also enough to help Amiya and I face the leader of the guerrillas...
'Patriot, of Reunion.'
Dr. Kal'tsit! The abandoned trolley station, behind the fallen restaurant sign, the gate of #4 Secondary School, third floor of the town hall clocktower!
Acknowledged.
Copy! I'll go to the town hall, Dr. Kal'tsit!
Then we'll go to #4 Secondary School, Doctor. Move.

opts: I'll help you command, Dr. Kal'tsit.

lines.x: Precisely.
Rosmontis, Amiya, and I will deal with the runners. And you...
Dr. Doctor, you will command our free units and slow the guerrilla fighters.
Remember, we target only the enemies we can target.
Once the guerrillas regroup, we fall back. We are here to complete our objective, not to entertain some fantastical idea of a direct fight.
This will be a protracted battle. We could suffer casualties, or even total defeat.
If that happens, we could be facing a new dark age for the Infected.
The best possible outcome for us could be that the command squad does not appear.
We head straight for the command tower and the generator area, leaving the Reunion Infected to handle their own problems with the Infected.
But he won't, not this guerrilla commander.
He won't lose control of the battle and watch his team fall apart. It is not faith alone that keeps his people together, he has the strength to live up to his name.
After this battle, if all goes well... we will commit everything to a conflict with their command squad.
We will face down Buldrokkas'tee.
Runner Three, down!
Runner Seven, destroyed!
Runner Eight, intercepted.
Raidian, execute the feint! Use your Arts to extend or hit their nervous systems directly!
Pull all the enemies!
*Pant* *pant*... so tough. Is everyone okay?
No problems yet... But... it's really intense. I've never seen combat on this scale before...
I'm due for a vacation if I make it back from this.
Right. I'll approve it.
But first... let's knock out this team! Then if you want a raise, a vacation, a specific assignment, I'll grant anything!
Incoming!
Don't get distracted! Back in formation!
A major firefight by the east exit? Stay calm, push ahead slow. Don't give them an opening!
These don't look like Ursus or Yan tactics. What's going on? Who are they?
After picking off Ursus soldiers for years, now we get ambushed?
No actual fighting...? Cowards! You'll pay for underestimating the guerrillas!
Watch the altar! The protection the Captain gave us only mitigates its effects. He told us a million times not to go near it unless we have to!
Runner, get the other teams on the horn.
...No response?
Where are the mortars? And the devils?
Hold on. Something's not right.
Runner Twelve, destroyed!
The guerrilla fighters are closing ranks! Now's our best chance to break through!

opts: No! It could be a trap!

lines.x: Roger. Rosmontis, let's scan the area with our Arts.
I can't reach it. The enemy... is blocking me somehow.
Sarkaz casters. They're the only ones who could interfere with Arts like Rosmontis's.
Then it's up to me...!
Raidian, how long can you keep it up?
...We'll be quick.
I... I found them. Yes. They have a lot of snipers and casters hidden in those buildings.
They're calm... some agitated. But they're not fanatics. They're... fortifying that point.
...Duty.
The Doctor is right. It's a trap.
I'm nearby. I'm coming.
Can you do it?
Yes. I won't smash the whole building. I'll just destroy the level they're on, so they won't bother us.
Amiya, I can do it.
Right, Rosmontis... I know you can. Definitely.
...Amiya, will you p'ei fu me then?
Err? What?
I heard Blaze say there aren't many people who can make you p'ei fu them.
P'ei fu? Oh... does that mean 'respect?' Sorry, I'm not so good with the Yan language. I'm pretty sure that's what it means?
I don't know, Amiya...
...Doesn't matter. Because I've always 'p'ei fu'd' Rosmontis.
You're amazing, really. I couldn't do what you do...
I couldn't bear that loss and loneliness.
There's no way I could live with it like you do, suffer through it like you do.
You're lying.
But it doesn't matter to me. Even if you're a liar, you're still Amiya to me.
Someone could... p'ei fu... a person like me?
It's like you just said, Rosmontis. You're Rosmontis, so I respect you. As both an elite operator and a friend.
Keep at it, Rosmontis.
Okay. We'll keep at it.
Where are they? What are they waiting for?
N-No! We can't leave the area! We strike as soon as they show their faces! No one else falls out of contact!
Hey, is that an enemy on top of that building over there?
...A cat? Just one little Feline...?!
Runner! Tell the casters! The enemy deployed a caster on the building across the way!
We've gotta take her out!
—A sword? A weapon? She's pulling them out of the air...
Huh? Floating weapons?
Slice from here, to there.
Hold it... with my hands. Cut, with the sword.
...Like slicing a potato.
Like slicing a potato.
Doctor! Get over here!

opts: The whole building... got cut in half?

lines.x: Rosmontis is destroying the guerrillas' ambush point. Watch for falling debris!
Assuming you're talking about Rosmontis's Arts, it may be time to explain it to you, now that you've seen it so many times.
It is a state of mind she shapes herself. You can think of it like Rosmontis has a gigantic, second, invisible body that cannot be acted upon.
It is a formless extension of her sense of touch. She can grab, squeeze, lift up, and toss away.
But these Arts lack objective manifestation, as opposed to her subjective mind.
It can act upon us with force, but we cannot act upon it. We only know it exists with the application of special Originium Arts.
You need not grasp it fully. This is not your area of expertise.
Rosmontis was much more destructive before we put a weapon in her hands. A lot like being crushed beneath the treads of a nomadic city. All that's left is a crying young girl alone in the ruins.
Under no circumstances can that be allowed to happen again.
...Doctor, move.
I see it. The command group is coming...
There should be a waterway under the ruins of the park fountain.
They hid in such an inconspicuous place.
...The story of the ambush on Graymane Peak, that supposedly involved seven straight days of stakeout and tricked the strongest of knights, may well have been true.
Let's go, Dr. Doctor. No time to lose.
Raidian, cease fire. Find a good place to hide. I need you to focus on keeping our comms up.
Mantra-2, log all our conversations. Every last word.
Runner Group Fourteen, successfully intercepted.
Amiya, Dr. Kal'tsit, I'm ready.
(Are you okay...? Talk to me! Look at me!)
(Good, you're with us. I'm okay. Doesn't matter. You hide somewhere, don't let them find you... we need you to regroup.)
(I know. We'll get them. You stay quiet!)
(I'm fine. No injuries. A little stone is nothing compared to an Ursus Fortcracker.)
(Don't panic! I can stop it, you look for an opening!)
You! What are you looking at?! Too scared to fight us?
Whoever you are, you lost the guts to take us on now? Too scared of Patriot's blood? Scared of his witchcraft?
Thinking back to the time you fought the Sarkaz? You had them outnumbered, you could've won, but in the end you just ran up the casualties!
But we'll do you one better! You can't break our shields, you can't crack our armor! You're nothing before the undying Patriot!
The mightiest Ursus warrior... needs no spear or shield. He'll stomp you to pieces.
You... are nothing!
Our fighters will regroup here soon! You can't run. Reunion will put you through hell!
Oppressors who enslave the Infected like us! You will be swallowed up by the Reunion movement!
Reunion—
...Reunion already betrayed you, sir.
—Little girl?
...Two... Infected little girls?
What are you doing?!
Out of the way.
There's a man behind you, and I'm going to break the device behind him.
You think I'll show you mercy just because you look like a kid?
Don't underestimate Patriot's Guard! We're the Aegis of the Infected... I'm here to stop your attack.
If you attack the Infected, I'll strangle you with my own two hands!
Then my weapons will talk for me.
You... You're the cat from before.
Your sword...
(Get out of here, guardian! Tell the others! I'll be okay!)
(That's your job, not mine! You have to survive!)
Come, try to pierce my shield! Try to break my armor!
Amiya. I'm out of options.
But...
He won't die. But he may never walk again.
Go. Stab...
You, can't.
How... you blocked my sword with your bare hands?
Hm... My hand... numb...
When did you get here? I never felt...
No. Oh... I think... I thought that was a building collapsing, but that was you?
You, no, you and your team... You're like...
...a mountain.
Rosmontis, fall back.
But I...
That's an order, Rosmontis.
An order.
Captain...!
You did, well. Go, regroup.
Now, you...
Rhodes Island.
I don't care, who, you are.
You attacked, my men. You, die.
Are you sure you don't care?
Very, sure—
Wait... wait.
You...
...Buldrokkas'tee.
You... how...?
It's been a while.
You... Dame Kal'tsit?
It's been a long time, Buldrokkas'tee.
Dame, Kal'tsit?
Why don't... you age?
Shieldguards. Guerrillas. Stand by. Hold position.
Roger.
Lord.
The way you talk... is that because of an infected organ in your larynx?
No. Just, over time, became like this. I can't, speak smoothly, anymore.
Dame... I never thought, I'd see you, again.
I remember, faintly. When my people, left. You and, Her Majesty, didn't stop us.
That was a long, long time ago.
I was, in my prime. Now I know, too sudden. I was wrong, about the King.
...You'll still call her 'Majesty' and 'King.'
A king, is a king.
When I left, she wasn't. Now, she has passed.
You still distinguish the King of Kazdel and the Emperor of Ursus.
Decorum. War does not, muddle, my thoughts. Who I served? All gone.
Ursus now, is my homeland, too. My liege, buried in Ursus. Vast land.
...To long, Dame. When we left...
My people were, not yet withered. Still, it was not, long.
But you... no change. The years, did not change you.
Not true. The years have changed us all, Buldrokkas'tee. I'm not the same woman I was before.
Buldrokkas'tee... why did you leave Kazdel?
Why I left, I think, you will laugh.
I left, because, I hated killing.
...But for my life, I have killed.
All that you've been through has only tempered your will. Your blood still flows in your veins. I will not laugh at you. You've earned respect.
High praise, Dame. I failed to avoid, killing. I did, kill so many.
Dame, this name... Do you know, how I got it?
Your followers consider you a true warrior, fighting for Ursus. You fight injustice, slavery, bloodshed and oppression for the future of Ursus.
Do you agree?
(Shakes his head)
Even if war, is necessary, such a title, is trouble.
For many, we started war. But war, is murder.
The war ends. If it, truly, ends. We should, all die.
But war never ends.
But I will die.
But, for my death, I want, meaning. I want to see, for Ursus, for the Infected, another future.
I asked, many times, of my people, Kazdel. You and, Her Majesty, worked together, for many years. She trusted you.
So, I too, trust you.
You led, Rhodes Island, here. Not for blood.
...But them, those two. They stink, of my daughter. Her death.
Mr. Patriot...
No. No more.
Do you seek vengeance on Rhodes Island, Patriot?
No, Dame. Not vengeance.
Why take, vengeance, for her, actions?
That was, her path. She chose. No matter, where it ended.

opts: Your daughter fought us with everything she had!
And she approved of us too.

lines.x: Quiet.
...Mr. Patriot?
She's, so young. Only knows, blind trust.
The truth, we believed, in the end, is exposed. Only a few, wolves, in sheep's clothing.
Mr. Patriot! Miss FrostNova gave her life, her Arts... everything...
Miss FrostNova let go of everything just to prove herself, her anger, her ideals...
Her life had meaning!
Enough. Not your place, to say this.
Oh...
(Amiya, don't. Say no more.)
What, do you know?
If my daughter, was not, terminal... Killing you, would be easy, for her. Like slicing wheat, with a sickle.
What you, endured, I do not care.
It means, nothing. It proves, nothing.

opts: Talulah has set the core city on a collision course for Lungmen!

lines.x: And is she, declaring it, Ursus territory?

opts: Seems like we can skip that part.

lines.x: Patriot... how... then why...?
The puppetmaster, has many tools. Puppetmasters, think alike.
But I cannot, let you through. Reunion, cannot destroy, itself.
I won't, betray again. Or they, the Infected, the struggle, are for nothing. They die.
Even if, Talulah, has gone mad...
How do I tell, the Infected, their leader, is mad? A traitor?
How do I say, she is wrong now, but once right? How do I say, she, who you follow, must now die?
I won't let, Reunion, become 'Infected, civil war, then nothing.' I will not.
But Reunion won't be able to carry its own weight. And besides, Talulah has been working secretly to destroy Reunion from within.
You are right, Dame Kal'tsit.
And so, you are here.
I prepared, to face, many types of foe.
But I did not, expect, in the end, to fight Infected.
...Is this really what you choose?
Buldrokkas'tee... You don't have to die.
Choose? I have only, one choice.
To kill you.
Patriot, we don't need to throw more lives away! This isn't like it was with Miss FrostNova! No matter who wins, this battle is playing right into the enemy's hands!
Wrong.
Cautus, this, is war.
You want me, to stop. Why, should I stop?
I fought far, too many wars.
As the, Wendigo, of Ursus... barbarians. Flayers, ripped flesh from bone, vicious herds, we cleansed.
Monsters. Twisted, stinking limbs, eyes in the, hundreds. Fingers in, the thousands. We slaughtered.
Warriors. Victoria, with steam armor. Kazimierz, with Silverlance Pegasi. Laterano, with Apostolic, Gun-Knights. All of them, we defeated, utterly.
But we cannot, defeat ourselves. Cannot defeat, the Empire's greed. Cannot defeat, our people's deaths.
We, the Wendigos, are the Sarkaz, closest, to death.
You are stained, with my daughter's death.
In this world, there are fates, worse than death.
Why should I stop? Who dares, test me? My virtue?
They called me, righteous. Should I, accept?
Fate thinks me weak. Do I suffer it? They called me strong. Should I bear it?
Traitor, rebel, butcher, guardian...
All of them, like 'Patriot.' On the battlefield, they are nothing, but scraps.
Rhodes Island...
You killed her. I'll kill you.
It is not, revenge. I have no heart, for vengeance.
My daughter just, gave up the fight.

opts: That's not how it is, sir.

lines.x: If not, then how? She gave her life, and you still live?!
I, will not, give up.
I only know, what I see. I only know, what I hold.
I don't fight you, for my daughter.
...But I loathe fate.
I will fell it, crush it, grind it to dust.
If fate, lets you, make me turn.
I would rather, never turn, back.
...Captain! Are you really going to...?
Anyone who, thinks me, dishonorable. Stand now, challenge me.
But if you, value your life... We part, here.
I don't want, for you, a foolish death. I won many times. I know this, too well.
You can't...
Buldrokkas'tee, don't be stupid, you can live on.
Dame, you too, have tasted loss.
And so, could you, stand by, and watch?
—You—
My blood, no longer, serves her. Yes. My country... is Ursus.
But when she, died. We heard it... that long, long gasp.
So you could feel it...
That was the moment with the pulse containing her Arts spread through the body of every Sarkaz in the world.
It was only an instant. But you felt it. That means your blood... is ancient enough.
So ancient that, I am alone.
I never thought, born in Kazdel, never Infected... Then, an old man, I tasted it, Oripathy.
Is that right?
We all know, Dame. We all know. Things in this world, do not happen, because they are, right.
In fact, most things, are mistakes, again.
My men said, I can fix them. In fact, they are too many. There is no, looking back.
Do you fight, to this day, for what, you lost, Dame?
I think, no.
I think, you lost, and you refuse, to lose more.
My daughter, has passed.
—I fight not, for her death. I fight, for the living, for all Infected, to the end.
Reunion, cannot fail.
Reunion must, liberate. All Infected.
Are you sending messages for him and his men?
What will you say to him?
...You're trying to set up the guerrillas. You're... you're going to turn the Ursus Infected against them, to kill them.
You know they won't raise a hand against the civilians.
Hate is the strongest weapon. It's a killer.
But he won't die.
He's Patriot... he's all the greatest legends summed up in one man. I've seen his armor. I've seen him swing a weapon.
He can't die! He and his people are the strongest fighters in all Reunion!
You want to kill the Infected. You devils never cared about life, but he does!
He's Ursus... he's Sarkaz, and he's Infected! But none of those qualities push him to do wrong!
He's not just Ursus, he's not just Sarkaz, he's not just an Infected. He's the Patriot, just the Patriot.
He'll beat you, he'll block your attacks, spit on your insults, regroup the Infected, and cleanse their hate...
He's Patriot, and he won't die.
And you're a bunch of maggots, dancing on the strings of the disgusting schemers running this show.
You're talking like that because you know you're gonna die.
And you're definitely right about that.
Patriot's pretty tough. But you're not. You're gonna die.
So you, will not pass. I will not, allow it.
To go through, central district, you have to, kill me.
Otherwise, my guerrillas, will hunt. And I, will be here, strangling you, to death.
You will die, at my hands.
Only if, you kill me, will your value, be known. Otherwise, you are only, intruders.

opts: I'm afraid I don't really follow.

lines.x: Of course, I don't believe, you have any, value.
I don't know, how you could, resist, my people's, witchcraft. But you will not, survive, Ursus tactics.

opts: You really hate us that much?

lines.x: I don't hate, anyone.
I just don't, trust you. I cannot, trust.
Even you, Lord. You brought...
You won't approve of us.
I don't believe, in coincidences.
War.
War, does not care. Not about right, or wrong.
It does not, care about, the suffering, or the oppressed, or the enslaved. Off the battlefield, there are. On the battlefield, there are not.
You must prepare, for the battlefield.
Whoever dies, is wrong.
I can't... I can't understand him...!
What is he saying... why do we have to fight?
Fight, or be crushed.
Resist, and be crushed.
Unless, you defeat, me.
If fate, stands with me, I kill you. Then I, shall kill it. So it cannot, mock anyone, ever again.
If fate, stands with you.
I can only, go forward. Advance.
All operators, fall back, immediately!
I step forward.
Our target is an Infected commander of the Reunion movement, code name: 'Patriot.'
His armor, his strength, his stature, far surpass anything you would expect of a warrior.
Originium may have taken over many of his physiological functions. The Originium in what was once his larynx... is a product of the high rate of crystallization happening inside his body.
That's the most we've been able to gather from our intel.
I have reason to believe that a typically fatal wound will not do any significant damage to him, as the Originium will grow and replace tissue damage!
Wendigos naturally exhibit an extremely high metabolic and repair capacity. And from where we stand, we don't know whether his infection is causing him pain, or is a benign mutation.
But no matter how resilient he may be, we have to kill him.
Only by killing him can we get through the central district, reach the bridge, and stop the enemy's exploitation of Reunion!
His guards have been confused by his actions. This is our last chance.
Dr. Kal'tsit...

opts: How did it come to this?

lines.x: ...We're just a fork in his road. All that we've built means nothing to him.
We're a choice just like 'go kill Talulah right now.' But I think that one carries more weight to him, might be worth further investigation.
To put it frankly, he's looking down on us.
Even with Dr. Kal'tsit here...
Even the Emperor of Ursus himself couldn't get him to bow his head, let alone me.
We'll have to use his death... to open his eyes, and make him see all that we've done, and make him realize that we're stronger than him.
But there's no way he himself could ever... change. Everything else, all that you value, is too fragile for him. It can't bear his weight.
Hellagur from Azazel misjudged this Sarkaz's character.
...No matter how noble Buldrokkas'tee may be, no matter how much he carries on his shoulders, he is an indomitable warrior, first and foremost.
FrostNova trusted you, so she gave the last bit of her life to you... But Patriot does not.
He doesn't trust. He only trusts himself.
(...Even outwardly.)
Prepare for battle. It all comes down to battle now.
—Only war can kill him.
Wise choice, Lord.
Guerrillas, regroup.
You have only, a few minutes left.
I say, no more.
I seek no, understanding. I only, win wars.
Defying all comprehension, the monstrously fearsome warrior stands before you.
But, you remember, there is another monstrous warrior who comprehends everything, standing right beside you...
Amiya grips your hand tightly.
Rhodes Island, I advance.
Mon3tr, hold!
(Screech)
Sniper operators, keep up the fire on his torso. His armor is already severely damaged. Casters, keep the other guerrilla fighters under control. Don't let them interfere.
(Wails)
Pull back, Mon3tr. You don't have advantage in close quarters! You can't stop him now!
The sword is slipping! Even if... I try to hold him, I can't move him. He feels like a landslide...
Focus the energy into a beam... focus... keep focusing... then aim the beam at his heart!
Hissss...
Fire! Now!
Grrr...
Even that wasn't enough to destroy him...
'Ever fight is to the death. Life on the line in every fight, for life will return to the blood, and live forever in another body...'
Vanguard operators, keep up the pressure... fall back. His own body will unleash the 'cannibalism' ritual, as if he himself is a gigantic witchcraft device...
Buldrokkas'tee, there are many Sarkaz who would not see a Wendigo like you sacrificed for nothing.
No. How can I, be sacrificed?
I can still, move. I can still, kill you.
My body, still hears, my commands.
I have not yet felt, the pain... misery, and regret.
Is it enough? Could it be? Enough?
Mr. Buldrokkas'tee... please, stop... stop this! Fighting more will only bring you more suffering!
Warrior.
A warrior, should carry, all of the dead.
I carry, their pain, their hate, their silence.
If I, stop...
They died for nothing.
Suffering? You should, suffer.
You cannot, defeat me.
Hnng...
Don't be sad, my love...
You won't remarry? Don't be like that, dear...
*Sigh*, but you won't agree with my yapping. You're stubborn as a stone. Have I ever convinced you of anything?
You'll keep fighting, won't  you?
Promise me, Buldrokkas'tee, promise me you won't die in battle.
My only hope is that you don't suffer any more. Just think, after I'm gone, you'll still have our family. Look, our little Gro.
He's so cute when he's sleeping.
With his two little red horns, that still haven't hardened...
This is life. It's the continuation of my life. A child is the continuation of all the life in our world.
I know you won't eat me. I know how much you hate Wendigo traditions.
But I do so wish to flow through your life forever.
And yet, life... is ever immortal.
You're withering away. All that sadness is going to destroy you. Our son needs you to take care of him. Lingering here will be the death of you.
...No, Buldrokkas'tee. You're not a cold-blooded monster.
Your love for me is true. Even a misanthropic woman like me, managed to return your love all the same.
You look surprised... Don't be. I love you, just as tenderly and generously as you loved me.
Buldrokkas'tee, my love... the family you'll come to have, is also my family.
That's how I love you.
Helen, I don't deserve your love.
I couldn't protect you. I couldn't, keep my word.
My family, I couldn't protect... any of them.
I don't deserve... love.
You trusted me. I broke my promise.
I broke my promise, but I live. And so the promise, still remains.
Because the promise, bears hate, and regret.
Not only, the dead. Feline, where is it? Your hate? Your regret?
You, carry it, as you go.
The people, of this city. The Ursus Infected. The world. Those who lost, their parents. The world. Those whose parents, did wrong.
They are all carried.
Father, I'm begging you! Don't stand with the Empire!
Orders? The Empire's orders are ridiculous!
No, I'm sorry, Father... I wasn't mocking you. I would never.
But the imperial decree is a cruel joke played on all of us.
My friend Hector was flogged alive on the bridge, just because they found out he had Oripathy!
Didn't you like his poetry too? Didn't you read them aloud at night and shed tears of joy?
He's dead! He's been sent down the dark path of death and left us forever, never to return!
He was a poet. Only the written word had the right to send him to his death!
The Empire's so-called infected decree is just a wicked intrusion into our hearts!
That kind of death, the death of the Infected, is a disgrace to both those who take life and who lose it!
Did the soldiers of the Empire win the Emperor's favor with this disgrace? It's ridiculous!
You were born in Kazdel, you grew up with Infected Sarkaz... I know you can understand the Ursus Infected too!
...Why are you so indifferent? Is your heart made of iron too? Or is it because you, as a still uninfected Sarkaz, have no compassion in your heart?
We're Sarkaz... who's more susceptible to Oripathy than Sarkaz?
Maybe when one of us gets infected, you'll know how absurd these crimes are!
Maybe then you'll leave your path of bloodshed and take a walk down the boulevard of mercy!
Fine, fine, Father...! You're the one and only Buldrokkas'tee! If Oripathy won't haunt a tough soldier like you, it'll just have to creep up on me!
When it's hovering over my corpse laughing at you, you'll finally realize your mistake!
Don't let yourself regret it, Father!
...Grrovae'zeeal, you were innocent. So innocent.
The Infected, was me. I hid it, from you.
Father, to the end, had eyes, and was blind. Father couldn't walk, the right path.
I... regret.
I truly, regret.
My son... Father killed you. Father let you, die for nothing.
Father... doesn't deserve, to be your father.
If I stop fighting, I betray them.
More... I see... more and more...
Mr. Patriot... You have so much regret... You never said anything? You never confided in anyone?
I can only bear, my regrets.
Mon3tr, get back from him!
I would think, why did, the King, as she was, end like that?
You won't answer. Her Majesty's death, to this day, is a mystery.
But I know, she never ran. Start to finish, she would only smile.
If I stop fighting, all resistance, means nothing. If I stop fighting, I ran.
I cannot stop.
We're not related, though? It's kind of weird to call you dad.
...You don't look so good?
No way! It's not because... my brothers and sisters are scared of me!
Hey, do you have any ideas? On how I can get them to stop hiding from me because of you?
If you do, I'll even call you daddy!
Huh? Threat-en? I don't know what that word means. You didn't teach me yet.
*Sigh*, you told me my brother learned everything really fast. But I can only go step by step... Where's my brother now?
Huh?! Why are you hugging me all of a sudden? You'll freeze!
...Are you crying? I felt something drip on my shoulder...
No? Everyone says you don't have blood or tears. Does that mean you don't cry?
But you're definitely crying, Daddy...
...I don't know what I was saying! I'm sorry! Daddy, are you crying because you're freezing and it hurts?
Daddy! Daddy! I won't say things like that anymore! And, and when I grow up... I'll make a medicine for you so you won't get frozen!
Then you won't cry anymore, right? I'm gonna grow up really fast!
Family?
Oh, you mean my brothers and sisters, and me, and you, we're all a family? Even my brother who isn't here?
Um... then, Daddy, you're everyone's daddy!
Then I'll learn from you, and avenge grandma! I'll kill all the bad people who bully us!
I can't say that?
Why?! You kill the bad guys every day, Daddy!
...What do you mean you don't want me to become like you? Aren't you a good guy?
How can my daddy be a bad guy?! That doesn't make any sense! Tell me! How are you a bad guy?!
I hate you, Daddy!
If I can't be like you, can I go to the city?
Um... Daddy... Can we really go to the city?
The candy fingers you were talking about... are they really good?
So I can't eat it if I'm like you...? Oh...!
I don't get it.
Daddy...?
I guess, it doesn't matter, I could be a good guy or a bad guy.
As long as I can be with you, Daddy, and my brothers and sisters... forever.
You're my family, I know. My closest family.
Obviously, the second is... the third is...
Yelena... Yelena... My daughter.
My daughter... Father, couldn't, do anything, for you.
I couldn't, do anything.
I owe you, too much.
Too much.
Too much, too much...
Cautus girl. You are, Rhodes Island's leader.
What is special, about you?
One of your people, told me of you.
Your warriors, are excellent. But only excellent, they still die.
Where is it? Show me, your worth.
You want to stop, the core city. But you can't, defeat me?
Don't touch Amiya!
One, two, three... four!
Shield, shattered.
Spear, snapped.
The huge monster doesn't even flinch. He keeps hulking forward.
Heh.
You can, pierce my armor. This suit, is finally, at its limit.
...You won't get away...!
Feline, you are strong. A threat, to me.
Wait, Mr. Patriot.
Only ten, more steps. No need to wait.
You're at your limit, I know.
Because you're not a caster like Miss FrostNova...
This is enough already.
I've seen everything... in these Arts. Everything of you.
...It's enough, Mr. Patriot.
It's enough.
What... is... this?
This strike, it's precise.
I can't, move, anymore.
...His wounds are finally catching up to him.
Buldrokkas'tee...
(I planned, to destroy you, then the guerrillas, in your name, kill Talulah.
(No need, now. You won, the right, to kill, Talulah.)
(Mr... Patriot?)
(How could I, let the Infected, be enslaved, exploited?)
(My life, take it. Use it. It's your prize, as the victors.)
You, won.
Dame. Please. Take this.
—What is it?
The key, to stop, Chernobog.
If you go, to the bridge... you need it.
Mr. Patriot, you... just to...
Oh...
No.
I did, intend, to kill you.
But now, you defeated me. You earned it.

opts: Why did you take it this far?

lines.x: Because I don't trust you.
I only trust, war.
My daughter, was naive. She was soft, she assumed, an outcome.
I don't. I wait for results.
And this... is the best... result.
My men, surprised, did not use, their strength. They live.
Only I, alone... will die. A worthy death.
...What happened?!
Closure, I need the schematics to Bioprocessing Unit Thirteen!
Cabin temperature is increasing rapidly! It's an energy leak!
Wait, it's changing shape! Why is it progressing like this after fourteen hours?
PRTS, who was in this cabin last?
Dr. Doctor? Did we lose someone in the battle for Lungmen? Who's being processed?
What?
Wait, slow down. Who did you say Dr. Doctor put in there?
Dame!
I'm listening, Buldrokkas'tee.
The Wendigos, exiled ourselves, lost everything.
But we carry, Sarkaz traditions.
Ancestors, molded me, nurtured me, watched over me. Now, bear witness.
Buldrokkas'tee...
...I don't know if I'm qualified.
I think you, are the best, choice.
'...I hereby witness a Sarkaz. Buldrokkas'tee, Wendigo of Kazdel, betrayed neither flesh nor blood, disgraced not his tribe.'
'He leaves his body, with his life, under the light...'
'...And his soul returns to the warm blood of the Wendigos.'
This is a curse.
It is not, to me.
Oh... I feel... I feel... My vision, begins to blur.
Thank you, Dame.
I thought you hated this stuff.
But I, am old.
No...
Where are we?
Ha. So that's how it is. Ha.
Talulah really did betray us.
You think we're just going to stand here and let you kill us?!
You can't fight.
If there's an afterlife, take your revenge there.
We're supposed to give up, just because we know we're going to die?
We're going to be killed for no reason and—
Don't kill them.
Let them go.
Mephisto...?
Devils, I'll give you bodies that know no pain. Let them go.
We're a lot tougher than your herd.
I'll share my life with you. And you'll really fight forever, a tireless fight.
...Fine.
But if we let them go, my people have to watch them. We're not about to disobey Talulah now.
You, crossbowmen. Go.
Mephisto?!
(Why would he save us...? Is this part of Talulah's plot?)
But why come here?
...Because I want to go in there. I want to know what secrets there are. I want to know why Rhodes Island would bring someone out of here.
Because I saw someone coming out of here on the logs.
Mephisto...? Why?
Would you like to hear a song?
But, Captain Faust said... He said...
Yes, yes. I can't sing anymore.
Oh, but I can try.
It ought to be a lullaby, yes? I don't remember who taught it to me... I've forgotten.
It's just, someone in my dreams was humming it.
Go to sleep, go to sleep♪
Hedgehog dolls and little bears...
The Ursus, Infected, where will, they go?
How many stars... does the dark night, hide?
...Captain, Captain!
Final order.
Captain...
Fight on. Live on.
Only you, walk... your own path.
To have a path, you must walk.
I will die. But I do not know, if I should die, now.
What is it, my death, can change?
Maybe you, can tell me, the answer.
Sink into the still dark...
Brothers, sisters, young, and old. Dreams, oh dreams, envelop their bodies...♪
Missy? What are you bawling about...?
I'm not bawling!
Fine, fine. But you're right in front of the door to L.G.D. Headquarters.
Probably don't want other people seeing you cry, huh?
N... Nobody's here anyway!
How about this? I'll sit on the steps in front of you.
I'm big enough. Nobody'll see you cry if I'm in the way.
What are you doing...?
Oh. Well, we screwed up, so we feel lousy, right?
I mean both of us, Missy. We both blew it, didn't we?
What do I care...? I don't care where she went!
Um, right. I know.
Hey, no hitting. You're going to hit me no matter what I say, huh?
Finally...
My... life... can end.
Helen... Grrovae'zeeal... Yelena...
It's you...
I'm finally... going home...
No.
—No.
No.
Hallucination.
How... I, don't need visions, don't need, consolation!
Who...?
Who shows me... hallucinations?!
Oh... How...? How did Mr. Patriot... break free?
Now, I see.
—You. Cautus. It's you.
You, are her... successor.
Theresis, lied. The King... had a... successor.
You are... the Lord of Fiends.
To die, at the hands, of... the King of Sarkaz.
I... how?
In the end, I could not, escape fate.
No. No...
No.
Amiya?! You can't...?!
S-Sorry! B-But Dr. Kal'tsit...
I heard, in the past, Lords of Sarkaz, gave visions of comfort, as favors.
Their champions, could see great walls, or loves long passed—
Countless soldiers, for these visions, fought ceaselessly.
I just, I only... Mr. Patriot, I didn't mean to... I didn't want...
...The end of your life... it shouldn't be so tragic!
I saw it... I saw your two hundred years of life...
The constant fighting... and so much loss... you lost everything...
I don't... I don't want that... to be your end!
My, end?
...No
I wanted to give you... a better...
No!
My end, is not, someone else's, to give!
A vision? No! That is not my end!
If it is tragic, then it is tragic. If foolish, then foolish!
This is, my end. The end... I deserve.
I lived. I fought. I failed.
It's, enough.
Why? Why...? Why is it enough?
No one should have to lose everything like this!
...You are still, a child.
I'm not a child! I've been in more battles than I can count!
...A good ending, is never earned. Only children, believe in, happily ever after.
You, and my daughter, are only children, who believe in, fairy tales.
I told FrostNova, too heavy... too heavy.
I disagreed, with her. She was... a young girl. Only a child.
Oripathy, slavers, our world... won't even spare, a child.
Talulah, sent her, to die.
Then why not join us and fight Talulah tog—
What can you, give me?
You cannot, even give, what Talulah, once gave me.
Later, what will you, become?
This is fate. I tasted, too much.
I will never, bow to fate.
Even if, every time, fate brings me, to the same... end...
I would rather, wrestle it, until again, the sun rises.
But... Maybe, you can change.
I fought fate, so many years... in the end, I could not win.
But you... Maybe you're, different...
Do you have, what it takes, to succeed the King?
Oh...
Do you have, the power, to leave, alive?
Do you have, the courage, and insight, to step into, the infinite, wilderness?
I can't do it alone.
But I'm not alone.
Maybe...
Everything... is yours. Only you... can do it.
That... Reunion, tyrant, fate... You will... overthrow them.
Wait.
No.
Kal'tsit looks up at the darkened sky.
Where have I seen this before?
It couldn't be...
Amiya... Amiya...?
The Sarkaz prophecy is born out of their collective racial memory. Originium energy bursts popping in areas with high concentrations... the core city is suffering a Catastrophe.
The altar... Amiya... Wendigo... The Lord of Fiends?!
Wait, if this has a direct affect... Impossible! The last of the ancient Wendigo bloodline is directly connected to the entire Sarkaz race?!
The prophecy... this prophecy?!
Snow? Buldrokkas'tee's cannibalization resonated with all the altars?! And what I just added... the Wendigos' ressurection...!
All operators!
Anything he says... Anything Buldrokkas'tee says...!
Do not believe a single word!
The Wendigo suddenly speaks, his hoarse voice seemingly echoing throughout the entire central district.
I see cities, devastated.
I see Originium, blanketing the land.
I see you, black crown on your head, melting millions of lives, into nothing but memories.
I see the, King of Sarkaz, enslaving all peoples, everywhere.
Impossible... No...
Patriot! Don't believe the prophecy! It's just a physiological side effect of the Originium Arts!
But... But I know... all the prophecies... come true.
And I died, at the hands of, the King of Sarkaz.
...Dame. No. Dame.
Buldrokkas'tee! Did you not fight against fate your entire life?!
The young, King of Sarkaz...
...You, you...
Will be the most horrific disaster to afflict our world.
She must die!
Or else, the world will die!
I cannot, let you live.
You can hate me.
The sound of clanking armor reaches your ears.
The ravaged monster suddenly reaches out to Amiya.
—Amiya!
W-Watch out!
The Cautus girl does not flinch. The shadow of death sweeps upon her smoke-blackened face. She does not move. She does not scream. She only stares directly into Patriot's eyes.
As if staring directly into his soul.
At the same time, heavy black blades and shimmering beams of ominous energy pierce swiftly into the giant's immortal body. Still, he remains unshaken.
Just as Patriot is about to crush the skull of the girl in black...
The Wendigo freezes in place.
Something appears to be dripping sorrowfully from his helmet.
A minute passes.
During this eternity of a minute, there are no other sounds except for the low rumbling of the moving city.
Then, the realization dawns. The monster standing before us is dead.
Having never taken a single step backward, unwavering for even a single second, in the end, death put a stop to the Patriot's lifelong advance.
You're hurt?!
Don't worry. Just a scratch.
How is that just a scratch? Come here, I have disinfectant wipes.
Look, I'm fine...
Turn your face this way!
Fine, fine.
...The wound is deep, but not very wide. It's as if... there's a trace of Arts.
...Did old Ch'en do this to you? That blasted woman...
Hah... It's fine. Girls of her age are bound to do stuff like this.
...Hoshiguma, I'm really scared. I'm scared that she'll...
That she'll join Reunion?
Yeah...
I also played with Talulah when I was young.
Even though I don't remember very clearly, I do know... that I really liked being her friend.
Even if she was bossy and had a temper, she always stood up for us.
She never said anything, but she never backed down. She was always the first to charge ahead, and never asked us for help...
She beat up kids who were older than her. She was the only one who could get Ch'en to snap out of her funks.
I'm afraid that...
She won't.
I'm telling you, Ch'en's heart is rarely on the same page as her mouth.
But, she will always be Ch'en of Lungmen. No matter what she might say, all her dreams, all her goals, her entire life... has always been right here.
So, why'd you try to stop her then?
...I was worried she'd get herself killed. Obviously.
Swire, you'll have an important role in the L.G.D. after this.
As if I could do that. I'm not her...
Oh, so you're admitting she's better than you?
I just don't think I'm cut out for the L.G.D.!
No, Missy, you absolutely are.
I'm not cut out for it. Neither is ol' Ch'en. Or Nine.
The one who really makes the L.G.D. shine... is you.
You have to take responsibility. You're the only one who can do it.
I'm jaded now, and there's a lot I don't see. I just want this city to become a place that accepts everyone.
I feel the same.
You—
It's been a long time, Miss Swire.
Hello, Inspector Hoshiguma.
Why is the esteemed daughter of the Rat King at this run-down place?
What are you planning to do to her? Let me say this, if you're planning to harm even a single hair on Missy's head...
I have no such intentions.
You... screwed up.
Maybe.
Sometimes, we have no choice.
I don't think there's ever a time... where we don't have a choice.
Look up in the sky.
It's going to snow. Can you choose for it to stop?
Why... is it snowing?
Go to sleep, go to sleep♪
Daddy's grey hair, Mommy's needles♪
Crap, we're exceeding the solubility threshold! Manually release the exhaust valve, now!
That bout of volatility just caused a violent heat-absorbing effect. If we don't increase emissions, the entire cabin's going to be ruined!
The rainclouds haven't moved much either... At this rate, we're in for some major snow.
This FrostNova... what kind of person was she?
All dreams sink to the bottom of the lake~♪
...'tis a reality that even time cannot shake~♪
*cough* *cough*...
Damnit... What an ugly song.
Damn it!!
Why was FrostNova so good at it? I really... can't sing well...
It's snowing...
This old man...
His crystallization rate is over sixty percent now. Even a Sarkaz shouldn't be able to move.
His consciousness has already dissipated... However, his iron will continues to ripple from his body.
...Buldrokkas'tee.
Resentment. Hatred. Remorse.
It's all gone now.
The only thing that's left is wrath.
The unwillingness to accept FrostNova's fate. The hostility towards Talulah's actions. The endless longing for Ursus and Kazdel...
A fiery and unquenchable wrath. For all the injustice around the world. For all those who fell around him... Towards those that would play with the fates of others.
To resist fate is to resist everything. Everything that contributes to the idea of 'destiny.'
Buldrokkas'tee's wrath... scorched this entire world.
...This entire, injustice-filled world.
...Mr. Patriot...
Move your hand.
You, Cautus Infected.
...We will complete the Captain's unfinished business.
...It's enough.
Do you want him to die for nothing?
He didn't want any of you to die. He wanted all of you to find another path.
He chose to die alone so that all of you could live...!
Who said we'll be the ones who die?
And we also heard what he said.
...Cautus. You're the source of all the ruin to come.
The ancient prophecies have all been fulfilled.

opts: But FrostNova put her faith in us!

lines.x: ...Still...
FrostNova... and now even the Captain...

opts: Of course he wouldn't just buy into FrostNova's assessment!
He just happened to ignore his daughter's opinion...
He didn't go into any of this on blind faith.
The decision he made... is truly just!
And you choose to believe some prophecy?

lines.x: ...No, it's not that we believe in the prophecy.
We believe in the Captain.
...What?!
Yelena... let you try her candy?

opts: I trusted her, and she trusted me.
If you really want to take someone's life...
At least be there to see it yourself!
You must follow your own will until the bitter end.

lines.x: No.
Rhodes Island, we get some of what you're saying. But we'll never see eye-to-eye.
We've followed the Captain our entire lives, but we we didn't do it blindly.
If... one day, that Cautus girl there turns into a tyrant like Talulah...
We will end you.
The Captain fought against all those people... against slavers, oppressors, corrupt rulers, and those who brought ruin. None could escape.
The Captain knew. About all the people in Chernobog who lost their lives to the Catastrophe. About all our battle brothers who died in vain.
He had his regrets, but knew he couldn't escape. He has set us free now, though. We belong only to the Captain and the ideals he fought for, not to Reunion.
We will take up his 'Aegis' from now on. Not as the Aegis of the Infected, but as defenders of all the enslaved.
We'll keep an eye on you, Cautus. If you take even a single step onto the wrong path, we swear to hunt you down.
We will treat these prophecies... as our secrets.
Because before that, we have a tyrant waiting for us in the control tower.
...The person who pushed you into a war against us and brought about this chain of tragedies is the leader of Reunion, Talulah.
We're going to regroup. We need to tell the guerrillas the news.
As for you... Continue to carry out your mission, if that's what you want.
Snow... flakes...?
This heavy snow... Is Yelena coming to pick up the Captain...?
Amiya, don't be too concerned about it.
The Sarkaz people have a long history with Originium. Their memories mix with Originium and become one with the land, returning to their own bodies.
Sometimes, certain tribes that have long passed down these Originium crystals will gather this information in one place.
Through the continuous use of Arts, this information, mixed together unexpectedly, manifests in different ways.
Most of these messages are lost to time, their contents jumbled or outdated. There is no basis for these prophecies.
But, they directly affect other people.
Like... someone hears these prophecies and starts to try to fulfill them, right?
I can't tell if these ideas are just vague whispers... or seeds that can bloom into reality. I really can't tell, but...
He could have killed me.
But... he thought...
I'll kill her now.
Stop. How are you so sure that she's our enemy?
She is Kashchey's heir! She'll poison us all, just like that old serpent!
No, you stubborn old man... You're wrong.
Even if she's bound to turn out like that in the end, we can't kill her now.
Because she's not Kashchey right now. Eh, who even is that? I can't understand you. All I know is, she's just Talulah.
...Who put these ideas in your head? It will twist your will to fight.
Twist? ...As if, old man. This whole idea of a pure warrior's spirit? If you really had that, I never would've met you.
If you really had that, we wouldn't be able to sit around a fire and have soup like we are now.
You're... just sixteen.
Did your soup get cold?
Always been cold. It's better that way.
I'll listen to you this time.
No, Father. That's not how it works.
If you believe you're right, then... no matter what happens, you should keep on believing that.
Because if something happens to Talulah, that's between her and her people.
But reality is just what's in front of us. Right, Father?
No matter what the future has in store, right now, I am I, and you are you.
No matter how much you dwell on it, we are who we are today. The only ones who we can change are ourselves, and we are the ones who will make that decision.
Let's try walking her path and see what happens.
...You'll be a great leader in the future.
I don't want to become a leader. I just want a comfortable life with my brothers and sisters, my uncles and aunts, and you.
I'll remember that.
Are you sure?
I won't forget.
Rosmontis... Dr. Kal'tsit... Doctor...
...He could've killed me... Mr. Buldrokkas'tee... could have killed me.
But I saw... I saw what he was thinking.
He didn't do it. He stopped himself, at the end.
I can still... hear him...
Amiya.
Tell me the most important thing. What was the last thing he said?
'I have seen. Woe and tragedy.'
'I have seen. Death and carnage.'
'I have seen. Neglect and blasphemy.'
'But someone once told me. It is all in our hands. If in the future, this is all that awaits us...'
'O, little King of Sarkaz. It is all in our hands. If this is fate...'
'Then I reject it.'
He said... He asked...
He sighed...
'My Lord... Where will you go?'
...Then nothing more.
Doctor... I've read it all. I've seen everything.
I read his entire life.
Amiya...
Buldrokkas'tee... He admitted that he betrayed his convictions twice in his life.
The first, was when he left Kazdel and walked away from his life as a Sarkaz warrior.
The second, was when he stood up against Ursus and walked away from his life as a warrior of the Ursus Empire.
And just now... I'd say he made his final betrayal. He betrayed Reunion and the Infected he served, and stopped being a warrior of the Reunion Movement.
With his death ends his last act of betrayal.
But...
He betrayed everything he cared about, because he has always stood up for his beliefs.
But this... isn't right, is it?
This kind of 'sublime betrayal' is a sort of release for those who deceive the world. It is an easy way to move things towards the best outcome.
But not for Buldrokkas'tee.
Do you mean that...
Speak up, Amiya.
...Kazdel is Mr. Buldrokkas'tee's home.
Even though he's come so far, he's always wanted to go back to Kazdel... It's not that he didn't want to go back home, but that he couldn't. He had to pay the price first... for his betrayal.
Ursus is his motherland. He fought for Ursus all his life... and blamed himself for the death of his son.
He believed that Ursus was evil, but for the sake of this land, he gave his life... fighting until the very end. He also paid the price for his second betrayal.
This time... he had to stand up against Talulah. But he foresaw that some of the people he strove to protect would die because of his actions.
Even though the decision he made preserved a future for the Infected, he... only felt pain... for the deceased.
This is his third act of betrayal, and the reason he was so determined to atone for it. From beginning to end, this battle was to make up for the consequences of his betrayal.

Through these three times, fate ended up pushing him further and further when he just wanted to turn back. He hated the cruelty and mockery of fate. He never wanted to follow it, but he bore the consequences for it all the same.
Ten years, twenty years... I'd be able to understand. But... too much time had passed. After facing all those regrets for so many years, too much time had passed.
At the very end, even I could not understand.
He could've taken my life, but he stopped at the end.
...He defeated his lifetime of suffering, his centuries-old ideology, and his love for this entire world.
He no longer followed the principles he had adhered to for countless years. He... succumbed to fate.
No, saying that would defile his memory. Rather, he chose to ignore everything he had been resisting, and overcame the final obstacle within his own heart.
He entrusted the future to you. This time, he gladly chose to do what he thought was right, rather than torture himself to maintain a certain ideal.
Of course, deep in his heart, this part is as you described...
It once belonged to Talulah, and now belongs to you. But it has always belonged to FrostNova.
Patriot... FrostNova...
Dr. Kal'tsit. Rosmontis... and Dr. Doctor.
I will end this war.
Right here. Right now.
Right.
I'll be with you, Amiya. Always.
Even if you can save many people, you'll cause immense suffering.
Anger. My anger comes from the injustice that fate has dealt to this world.
After countless years... are things finally coming to a head?
How could something like that happen? This prophecy... is it not just another fragment of Sarkaz history?
Core City Warehouse District
Why is one of Patriot's men over here? This isn't your patrol area.
You, let him go.
Ngh...!
I'll let it slide this time. A little warning, don't stick your nose where it doesn't belong next time.
I just received news.
...Patriot has passed away.
What?!
That's some big news.
So, right now... we have no more reason to keep up this charade.
Your band of devils smells far worse than any of our Sarkaz.
What happened? How did Patriot die?!
Take care of this Reunion scum first!
You're picking a fight with the core city?
What's wrong with that?
...Even Patriot is dead. You bastards... I won't tolerate you any longer!
If Patriot is dead, then what value does Reunion even have?!
Guerrillas! If there's anything I can do to help... tell me!
First, we're going to throw everyone who would toy with the lives of the Infected... right off these city walls!
Mantra-2, put on the uniform. Closure has already hacked the identification code, so make sure not to reveal your hidden weapons.
News of the incident should have spread already. What we need now is...
Chaos?
No, order. Go help the guerrillas. Let the Infected, the well-intentioned Reunion members, and those who fight against injustice rally under one banner.
Just as if Talulah was unable to engineer Patriot's death.
What...? What? What did he say?
Patriot is dead!
The one who was truly looking out for Reunion! The one who fought for all of us! Patriot is dead!
Talulah orchestrated this conspiracy against Patriot! ...She's an agent of Ursus!
Who'd believe your nonsense?!
Wait... Talulah has been acting strangely. She gave an order forbidding us from maintaining communications in the city.
What does that prove?!
It proves... that the mercenaries are going to ambush the guerrillas! I see their plan... those lunatics!
You—
Stop! Don't fight us, if you value your lives. Otherwise, we'll destroy you!
We are the Aegis of the Infected, and we'll battle every last enemy for our peoples! Even if we have to stand against Talulah!
For Patriot!
For Patriot!
Things have gotten a bit messy. You should leave soon.
What about you?
Us mercs have our own priorities. We don't care about Talulah's plots. Do your best, and live on.
For the future of the Infected!
For...
...Captain Faust!
For all our comrades who've been betrayed by Talulah! For Faust!
Patriot is dead! Patriot is dead! Talulah betrayed Patriot! Talulah betrayed us all!
Are you sure it wasn't an outsider that killed him? I saw someone else kill him, not Talulah!
Patriot plotted with the Ursus Army to try to kill the Leader! Open your eyes and see the truth! Patriot is the traitor!
Patriot must have foreseen this.
But, his struggle against the storm was in vain. Even the 'Aegis' have adopted his most radical ideas, and not his pragmatic approach.
After all this, the core city should descend into chaos. The Reunion Movement no longer exists.
Rosmontis, is this the revenge you wanted?
No, not yet. Not like this. I haven't seen the target yet. No...
No matter who you face, the outcome is the same.
Reunion is just a symbol. No matter who is caught up in it or what they do... they are not an individual. They act as a part of Reunion.
Revenge... will only get you this far. We cannot distinguish their faces. We can only eliminate the symbol itself.
Rosmontis, you can continue to carry out your wishes. But... do you find this kind of vengeance satisfying?
Kal'tsit...
Am I wrong?
I... I just...
It would be irresponsible of me to give you the answer.
Only you have the ability to pursue the things that you hold to be true. Rosmontis, I might not be the person who can answer that question.
Anger is entrenched into our bodies. Do we want to indulge ourselves, to let it flow and condense into hatred?
Or, can we turn it into a higher judge that measures all our future actions and illuminates our sanity?
Do you want to distinguish between right and wrong, or do you want to understand the reason why everything happens?
The lives and deaths of our operators, the sights that shake you... and the reasons why we feel these emotions.
Even if there are certain things that your mind erases, Rosmontis, they will always remain within you...
Maybe there are very few things in our lives that actually come down to right or wrong. Remove all these symbols, and only then do our lives have value.
Kal'tsit.
I'm listening.
I want to know the reason.
Dr. Doctor. We'll split up for the next part.
Rosmontis and I will go to the control tower. Dr. Kal'tsit will take you to the generator area.

opts: I won't see you for a while?

lines.x: It's just a few hours, right?
By the way, Doctor...
Even if I'm not at your side, I'll always know that Dr. Doctor is out there... fighting together with me.
Even out of sight, we still fight for the same goal.
I'm pretty happy with that.
Right.
Madame Ch'en may already be near the command tower. She's always been quick, so we'll have to pick up our pace.
Doctor... Promise me that you'll stay safe.
Our work here only means anything if we make it back to Rhodes Island safely.
Be careful, okay?
Chernobog Core City, Power Supply Sector

opts: Why'd you pick me for your team?

lines.x: Our decapitation strike team will be attacked by Reunion from all directions as they approach the control tower. I can't just drop you off in a battlefield like that.
When we were fighting the guerrillas, we could retreat and buy ourselves time to regroup. That won't be possible for their team. They won't even have an opening to catch their breaths.
This battle has a clear objective, and Amiya and Rosmontis's team can no longer afford any distractions.
You'll be safe with me.

opts: I don't feel safe around you.

lines.x: We should only take on responsibilities that we can fulfill.
After entering the sarcophagus, our communications will only further deteriorate.
At that time, we will truly be fighting alone.
Wait...
Doctor, please stay hidden for a while.
...If you don't want to, or if you're feeling claustrophobic, I won't stop you.
Ah... *cough* *cough*...
Where am I? How'd I blast my way down here... Is this it?
Huh? You're...
...Rhodes Island? What rotten luck I have today...
Hm?!
...What an unexpected reunion, Kal'tsit.
...You're gravely wounded.
What do you go by now, W?
Still W. Didn't have time to change it yet.
Hold up, why are you casually milling around a place like this? Don't tell me...
The old man's gone, huh?
Yes.
He died? How is that possible?
But... then again, he wasn't going to survive this. He never backs down, and never lets anyone through.
You sound a bit heartbroken.
Guess so.
That old fella was the only person who made me even give Reunion a second look.
Guess today... was that idealist's last.

opts: I saw him draw his last breath.

lines.x: Kal'tsit, story time.
Do I have something to tell you?
You're asking me? You kidding? How do you have the nerve to stand there all calm like nothing ever happened?
The Sarkaz woman glares at me with eyes that I had never seen before.
Though she directs her words at Dr. Kal'tsit, her eyes are fixed on me.
Kal'tsit, you're working with Dr. Doctor?
